title: Evidence Record
description: A single verified evidence record in a problem pack. Every claim that enters canonical truth requires one or more evidence records. The burden of proof sits with the submitter — reviewers need enough information to attack the claim.

id.description: Unique identifier for this evidence record. Use kebab-case. Example: who-malaria-fact-sheet-2024

problem_id.description: The problem pack this evidence belongs to. Must match the id field in problem.json. Example: climate-health/malaria-early-warning-africa

claim.description: One specific, falsifiable statement this source supports. State what the source can prove and at what grain. Do not state what it cannot prove here — use the limitations field for that.

evidence_type.description: The type of evidence. Use the most specific type that applies.

source.description: The source document or dataset. Use the original source, not a secondary report about it.

title.description: Full title of the source document or dataset. Do not abbreviate.

url.description: Direct, stable URL to the source. Prefer DOIs, archived URLs, or official permanent pages over search results or blog posts.

source_date.description: Publication or last-updated date of the source in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). If only the year is known, use YYYY-01-01 and note the uncertainty in limitations.

access_date.description: Date you accessed the source in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Required because URLs and content change.

method.description: How you assessed what the source proves. Describe which sections you reviewed and what analytical steps you applied. A reviewer should be able to replicate your assessment.

limitations.description: What this source cannot prove. Be specific: name the grain, geography, time period, or causal claims that exceed what the source supports. 'Results may vary' is not a limitation.

confidence.description: Your confidence that this evidence record accurately represents what the source proves. Not confidence that the claim itself is true in the world.
